statement.p: There is a rectangular lot in the garden, with {5\ {\rm ft}} in length and {3\ {\rm ft}} in width. You plan to expand the lot by an equal length around its four sides, and make the area of the expanded rectangle {63\ {\rm ft^{2}}}. How long should you expand the original lot in four directions?
You should expand the original lot by in four directions.

solution.p: Assume you will extend the lots four sides by x feet. According to the diagram, the expanded rectangles length will become 5+2x feet, and its width will become 3+2x feet. The new rectangles area will be (5+2x)(3+2x) square feet. Its given its new area is {63\ {\rm ft^{2}}}, so we can write and solve an equation:

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned}[t] (5+2x)(3+2x) \amp = 63 \\ 4x^2+16x+15 \amp = 63 \\ 4x^2+16x - 48 \amp = 0 \\ 4(x^2+4x - 12) \amp = 0 \\ 4(x+6)(x-2) \amp = 0 \\ x+6 \amp = 0 \text{ or } x-2 = 0 \\ x \amp = -6 \text{ or } x= 2 \\ \end{aligned} }

solution.p: We could solve this equation by the Quadratic Formula. Identify that a=4,b=16,c=-48.

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned}[t] x \amp = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^{2}-4ac}}{2a} \\ x \amp = \frac{-(16) \pm \sqrt{(16)^{2}-4(4)(-48)}}{2(4)} \\ x \amp = \frac{-16 \pm \sqrt{1024}}{8} \\ x \amp = \frac{-16 \pm 32}{8} \\ x \amp = \frac{-16 + 32}{8} \text{ or } x= \frac{-16 - 32}{8} \\ x\amp =2 \text{ or } x= -6 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: We dont take the negative solution as it doesnt make sense in this context.
You should expand the original lot by {2\ {\rm ft}} in four directions.
